content.full_text:
RA”I MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
July 5,
1995
Lysa Pisarski
Deputy,
Campaign Reporting
Orange County Registrar of Voters
P.O.
Box 11298
Santa Ana,
CA 92711
Re:
Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1-95-200
Dear Ms.
Pisarski:
You have requested confirmation and clarification of
telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”)
1
QUESTION
Must officeholders who receive compensation of $75 per
meeting and who usually attend only one meeting per month but
every third month attend two meetings file campaign statements as
“elected off iceholders”?
CONCLUSION
The members of. the boards of dirétors of the water districts
referred to in your letter receive an ave-rage monthly salary of
$100 and are required to file semi-annual campaign statements.
Pre-election campaign statements and additional special reports
may also be required when an elected officer is involved in an
election.
-
-
FACTS
-
-
Two water districts are located in your county which
compensate the members of their boards of directors in the amount
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code Unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
et .
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


.-_l_.J_
I
.1_.l:(
No.
1-95-200
Page
2
of $75 per meeting.
Ordinarily, only one meeting is held per
month and every third month two meetings are held.
CONCLUSION
Section 84200 requires elected officers to file two semi
annual statements each calendar year.
An exception is made for
elected officers whose salaries are less than $100 per month and
who do not make or receive contributions or make expenditures
during the semi-annual period.
2
Given your facts,
in any six-month period the board members
will meet once a month four times and will also meet twice a month
in two months.
Therefore,
they will always meet a total of eight
times every six months.
That means that they will always receive
$600 for each six months of service and therefore they must file
statements as officeholders.
In the Goudie Advice Letter,
No. A-91-303,
the district paid
$50 per meeting and ordinarily met only once a month.
However,
the district would infrequently have two meetings in one month.
Because the district was only paying $50 per meeting,
unless they
had two meetings every month,
the members of the board would never
receive an average of $100 per month over a six-month period.
Therefore,
our advice was that the members of that board “would
not be required to file a semi-annual campaign statement unless he
or she receives contributions or makes expenditures as defined in
Sections 82015 and 82025, respectively,
during the semi-annual
period.”
If you have additional questions, please contact
me at
(916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Wayne Urmberi
Political Reform Consultant
Enclosure
-
-
-
2
Another exception is provided for elected officers who will
receive contributions totaling less than $1,000 and who will make
expenditures totaling less than $1,000 during ‘the entire calendar
year.
These officeholders may reduce their filing obligations by
filing one Candidate and Officeholder Campaign Statement
- Short
Form
(Form 470)
for the entire calendar year.
(Section. 84206,
Regulation 18406.)

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must officeholders who receive compensation of $75 per
meeting and who usually attend only one meeting per month but
every third month attend two meetings file campaign statements as
“elected off iceholders”?

CONCLUSION: The members of. the boards of dirétors of the water districts
referred to in your letter receive an ave-rage monthly salary of
$100 and are required to file semi-annual campaign statements.
Pre-election campaign statements and additional special reports
may also be required when an elected officer is involved in an
election.
-
-

Box 11298 Santa Ana, CA 92711 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. 1-95-200 Dear Ms. Pisarski: You have requested confirmation and clarification of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) 1 QUESTION Must officeholders who receive compensation of $75 per meeting and who usually attend only one meeting per month but every third month attend two meetings file campaign statements as “elected off iceholders”? CONCLUSION The members of. the boards of dirétors of the water districts referred to in your letter receive an ave-rage monthly salary of $100 and are required to file semi-annual campaign statements. Pre-election campaign statements and additional special reports may also be required when an elected officer is involved in an election. - - FACTS - - Two water districts are located in your county which compensate the members of their boards of directors in the amount 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code Unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et . Ordinarily, only one meeting is held per month and every third month two meetings are held. CONCLUSION Section 84200 requires elected officers to file two semi annual statements each calendar year. An exception is made for elected officers whose salaries are less than $100 per month and who do not make or receive contributions or make expenditures during the semi-annual period. 2 Given your facts, in any six-month period the board members will meet once a month four times and will also meet twice a month in two months. Therefore, they will always meet a total of eight times every six months. That means that they will always receive $600 for each six months of service and therefore they must file statements as officeholders. In the Goudie Advice Letter, No. A-91-303, the district paid $50 per meeting and ordinarily met only once a month. However, the district would infrequently have two meetings in one month. Because the district was only paying $50 per meeting, unless they had two meetings every month, the members of the board would never receive an average of $100 per month over a six-month period.
